title: My Rage Is Information, Not Failure
subtitle: An affirmation for the maternal anger that feels like proof you are doing it wrong
excerpt: A short affirmation for the maternal anger that women are told to suppress or hide. Reframes rage not as evidence of poor mothering but as information: a signal about what is not working, what is needed, or what has been ignored for too long.

content.text: A short affirmation for the anger that motherhood produces and the culture says you are not supposed to feel. Maternal rage is real and it is not evidence of bad mothering. It is a signal that something needs to change, that something has been absorbed for too long, or that the load is not sustainable. The affirmation does not condone the action the rage takes. It names the rage itself as legitimate information.
Say it after the moment has passed and the shame has arrived. Three times, slowly.
My rage is information, not failure. I listen to what it is telling me.

After saying it, sit for one minute and ask: what was the rage trying to say? Not what caused it in the surface-level sense. What has been building. What is not working. What the body and the mind are trying to get your attention about.
